# VBOG — Depesh Vyas, Fractional COO

> Operational strategy and systems for service business founders at $200K–$1M annual revenue.

VBOG is run by Depesh Vyas, a Fractional COO who helps service business founders remove themselves from day-to-day operations and build the systems needed to scale. The site covers operations, hiring, SOPs, growth case studies, and COO engagement details.

## Blog Posts

- [Why Your Service Business Is Stuck at $20K MRR (It's Not a Sales Problem)](https://v-bog.com/blog/stuck-at-20k-mrr.html): Most founders think slow growth is a sales problem. It's not — it's an operations ceiling. Here's what's actually keeping you stuck at $20K MRR.
- [The Founder Bottleneck: What It Costs You to Be Your Own COO](https://v-bog.com/blog/founder-bottleneck-cost.html): When you're both founder and COO, you become the bottleneck. Here's how to calculate exactly what it's costing your business to keep you in operations.
- [COO vs OBM vs VA: What Does a $10K–$40K MRR Service Business Actually Need?](https://v-bog.com/blog/fractional-coo-vs-obm-vs-va.html): OBM, VA, or COO — the wrong hire at the $10K–$40K MRR stage costs you months. Here's how they differ and which one your business actually needs right now.
- [Signs Your Operations Can't Handle Your Next 10 Clients](https://v-bog.com/blog/signs-operations-cant-scale.html): Closing clients before operations are ready leads to delivery collapse and churn. Here are the warning signs your operations can't handle your next 10 clients.
- [From 0 Systems to $120K MRR: How a Marketing Agency Built Operations That Scaled](https://v-bog.com/blog/agency-0-systems-to-120k-mrr.html): A B2B marketing agency at $15K MRR had zero operational systems. Here's how we built the structure that scaled them to $120K MRR in 10 months.
- [What a COO Actually Does in Month 1 (A Detailed Look)](https://v-bog.com/blog/what-fractional-coo-does-month-1.html): Most founders have a vague picture of what a COO does. Here's an honest, week-by-week breakdown of exactly what happens in Month 1 of a COO engagement.
- [The $500 Operations Audit: What It Finds and Why It Matters Before You Scale](https://v-bog.com/blog/the-500-operations-audit.html): Before scaling begins, the $500 Operations Audit identifies what's broken and what to fix first. Here's what it covers and how to get started.
- [How to Remove Yourself From Day-to-Day Operations in 30 Days](https://v-bog.com/blog/remove-yourself-from-operations-30-days.html): Most founders say they can't step back from operations yet. Here's a 30-day framework for removing yourself from day-to-day execution without breaking things.
- [Why Most SOPs Fail in Small Service Businesses (And How to Build Ones That Stick)](https://v-bog.com/blog/why-sops-fail.html): Most service businesses have SOPs no one uses. The documentation exists but behaviour hasn't changed. Here's why SOPs fail — and what actually works.
- [What to Outsource vs. Hire In-House at $10K–$40K MRR](https://v-bog.com/blog/outsource-vs-hire.html): At $10K–$40K MRR, every hiring decision is high stakes. Here's a clear framework for deciding what to outsource vs. hire in-house at this stage of growth.
- [Case Study: From $5K to $220K MRR in 19 Months — The Operations Playbook](https://v-bog.com/blog/case-study-5k-to-220k-mrr.html): A B2B marketing services business grew from $5K to $220K MRR in 19 months with 180 clients and 40+ staff. Here's the full operational case study.
- [Case Study: Saving a 40-Year-Old CPA Firm From Operational Collapse](https://v-bog.com/blog/case-study-cpa-firm.html): A 40-year-old CPA firm faced a full team walkout, $150K revenue at risk, and tax season starting. Here's how operations were stabilised in weeks.
- [What a Full COO Partnership Looks Like (And What It Costs)](https://v-bog.com/blog/full-partnership-what-it-looks-like.html): Not all COO engagements are the same. Here's exactly what a Full COO Partnership looks like — structure, profit share model, and what to expect month by month.
- [Is a COO Right for Your Business? A 10-Question Self-Assessment](https://v-bog.com/blog/is-fractional-coo-right-for-you.html): Before committing to a COO engagement, answer these 10 questions honestly. They'll clarify whether your business is ready and if a COO is the right move.
- [The $2,300 Trial: Why We Start Small and What Happens in 30 Days](https://v-bog.com/blog/the-2300-trial.html): Most COO engagements demand a 12-month commitment upfront. The $2,300 trial is a 30-day integration before any long-term commitment. Here's how it works.

## Key Pages

- [Homepage](https://v-bog.com/): Overview of VBOG's fractional COO services, who it's for, and how engagements work.
- [Blog Index](https://v-bog.com/blog/): All articles on operations, scaling, and systems for service businesses.
- [Operations Audit](https://v-bog.com/operations-audit/): Details on the $500 operations audit — the entry point for most engagements.
- [Contact](https://v-bog.com/contact/): Get in touch with Depesh Vyas to discuss your business situation.
